Thank you.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, Tiffany, what's up?
Hi, Panzer, um, so this isn't, like, a workflow or a bug or anything, but I just wanted to say, can we get a little more, um, color?

In Atlas, because, like, it looks like, it looks like linear, and linear is daunting, so, yeah, I love the color in the home page, because I don't think it was there when, no, no, they just added it yesterday, so.
Yeah, so, I mean, like, in the pipeline, and artifacts, can we just, that's what makes Airtable so fun, so can we just get that?

Yeah, I would put it on the list, it's definitely going to have to go into, like, future feature requests, definitely not, you know, bug fixes, we've got a lot going on, but I actually agree, just from a usability perspective, I think we should, at the very least, have alternate shaded cells, and things like that, accessibility is a big one, Jen, obviously, but also, just having the ability to differentiate between, you know, one cell and the next, just the way human brains work, they use color to identify the difference between objects.
It's a hunter-gatherer thing, you know, so, I will definitely mention that.
There's a lot of UX stuff that I would follow behind, like, does this work, that I think we'll do a lot of cool work with it eventually.
I mean, the theory is, remember, eventually this is going to be a product that other people use, not just us, right?
So, we're building a product now that will be used by the public, and therefore it will probably change a lot between now and then.
Cool.
Anything else?
All right, all right.
Yeah, so, hey, hit me up if you have any blockers, any issues, don't stay silent, especially if you're unable to, like, get to it.
Actually,
See, I'm going to be a little, like, very minorly hardcore on this one for just a second.
If you, like, please have at least one fully generated article by the end of the day today.
Like, just something.
Even if you don't love it, like, make sure your grid flows all the way through and you have something to look at at the end.
That's just a kind of a minimum thing that we can then start debugging backwards in what's working and what's not.
Because I know some of you, because I can look at all of them, I can see them all.
I know some of you haven't actually run them all the way through yet.
Please get them all the way through.
And if you're blocked at any point in that process, then flag it.
Because I just want you to get to the final step so that then we can start judging.
Okay, I got to the final step, but it sucks.
Okay, that's cool.
Like, we can work with that.
But I think some of you haven't actually run all the way through.
Please run at least one row all the way through today so that we know whether you have any big issues or blockers.
Because we're about to, like, go back into production weeks in your client.
And so everybody's expecting work, and I want to make sure you're unblocked, at least to some degree.
Please.
Can I just ask, what's with the output?
Why does it remain empty?
So once we run the internal link, the output remains empty?
Is that okay, or is that a bug as well?
Yeah, no, I actually don't know if it's a bug.
I was actually able to get, have success having the output.
I'm almost positive that it's just the bug that I mentioned earlier in this call where you have to click away from it and click back, and a refresh also works.
So double check and make sure that your output isn't just blank because it's not refreshing, it's a rendering error, and not actually not putting anything there.
Okay.
But I need to file a bug on that, output, it's really the output field, because it's the same output field as on every step.
And I'm almost positive it would be a rendering issue with that field.
No, I don't think, I think that we are thinking about two different things.
I'm thinking about the last step in the workflow.
Yeah.
After the...
Mine appeared.
So, I get you.
I'm saying mine do appear there.
And if yours aren't, I doubt that it's not appearing there.
I think it's more likely, more than likely it's a rendering error.
Nothing is there.
Yeah, okay.
Thanks.
But, we'll check on it.
Which one specifically was it, by the way?
Which one?
Today, I was trying on Yurko.
There is my test space, so you can see it there if you're looking.
We also have a meet later on, so I can show you then.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
We can talk about it later, but I'll follow that above there.
And I will mention that it's the final output as well as some other output fields.
Because, as you saw earlier, and I was tabbing back and forth between those, like it's blank.
You tab away, tab back.
tabbing tabbing
and it's filled out, that's definitely a rendering bug, and then if that's separate or different from that final step not appearing, then we'll figure that out, but that's understood.
So you're talking about like the internal links, the output from the internal links, or the output output, because yours shows here for me.
No, so the step after the internal links, yeah, now it shows because I think I copy-paste it from my internal links.
Okay, got it.
Yeah, so if you hit this and it's blank, try refreshing just in case.
Okay, didn't try that, we'll go in the future.

Yeah, and so it should be there, right, absolutely should be there, if it's not, let me know, because yeah, that output page is important, because it allows you to download, you know, any of your formats.
I wonder if the output page is really where the metadata should live?
Elvis, like, I'm not really sure that it needs to have its own step.
Like, it should just be generated and then appear in your outputs so that you can access it when you're publishing or exporting.
Moses, yeah.
Moses, so my only concern is, would, from, let's say from now to next week, Monday, is the timeframe that's long for there to be, like, a significant improvement with Atlas.
For example, like, we are going to be fully working on our talks by next week.

Imagine publishing, trying to publish about 20 articles next week, and you wouldn't really have the time to be sending tickets in linear, trying to give feedbacks on the Splat of some, like, would there be a significant improvement by next week, Monday, where we don't really have to, like, complain much?
Um, I don't know.
I guess we'll find out, right?
Um, there, there's...
Fipping bug fixes every day, but that's why it's so important for you to flag every single thing and file any ticket you notice here now so that we can improve as much as possible.
I mean, the devs are cranking.
You know, they just had another big release notes this morning.
You should read the patch notes that Daniel's dropping to see what's being improved.
There was about 50 different fixes that went in just yesterday.
So filing tickets as fast as you can now and for everything that you see is super vital.
Because if we get to next week and you're like, oh, I can't because I noticed this thing last week.
It's like, why the hell didn't you file a ticket?
Pardon me.
Why didn't you file a ticket last week?
I think that's a really important thing to do.
We'll see where we're at, right?
I think there's a pretty good chance that you're going to be able to get to at least rough parity of Aerox by then.
There's not really much here that wasn't or much in Aerox that wasn't here aside from anything in your flow that's missing.
That's why I'm asking about those things.
So if you're finding anything in the pipeline that's missing, please let me know.
And then aside from that, the back end is the same back end we were using in Aerofs.
So if you can reliably run your pipelines from beginning to end, it should provide you with the same results that you got out of Aerofs because these are the same behind the scenes.
This is all about the way that the front end interfaces with them.
So if you can't completely run, please flag, right?
Or if you're getting wildly unreliable results, you know, that you're unable to tweak, even with a little external help for now, let us know, right?
We'll dive in and fix it.
Aerofs will stop working any minute, by the way.
So it is what it is.
I mean, not today, but I mean, like, maybe today?
I don't know.
So we have no guarantees.
So our contract is over with them.
That's when they decide to shut us off.
So we're not relying on them.
Um, sweet.
Anything else?
Yeah, we'll figure out how get this group.
I hope, that goes live today.
And then if you immediately start running it, and then we could look and see what needs to be adjusted there.
But hopefully the latest stuff is ported over from Air Ops.
As an insurance policy, I would take whatever your latest artifacts are in Air Ops and just save those locally for yourself.

Just in case the export, because since we're on a short timeline with Avengers Group, because we, it took so long to get them ported over, I don't want any weird issues where you're like, oh, this is not what, you know, my workflow was at all.
So go grab those artifacts and then, um, yeah, yeah, cool.
Okay.
Yeah, thank you.
That's good.
Very practical, I appreciate that, yeah, that's good.
Sweet.
And then, yeah, Moses, like, if you are really, really worried about what you're going to be able to deliver on Monday, do you still have air ops access for your client?
Or do you, are you still on?
Go generate, you know, go generate five, six, articles or whatever with the, know, just so have a little bit of a pad.
But I don't want to, like, rely on that anymore when you have to because it's going to go away at any moment.
But if you get a few extra to pad for an insurance policy, if you're really worried, you know, it will hurt your anxiety levels, I'm sure.
Cool.
All right, all right, anything else?
All right, all right, all right.
Okay, all right, I'm here, so if we run into issues, let me know, or talk to each other, use the learnings channel to share, and we'll go, onwards.
Ciao.
Yeah, you bet.
Yeah, you bet.
bet.
bet.
bet.
bet.
bet.
Yeah,

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Daily standup to discuss Atlas workflow updates, issues, and next steps for content management system (CMS) integrations.](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [New AI editing tool rolled out; experiment and report issues](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=511.0)
  - [Run full workflows end-to-end today to identify blockers](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=2225.0)
  - [CMS integrations (e.g. Webflow) in progress; prioritized by client usage](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=1963.0)
  - [File tickets for any Atlas issues to help rapid development](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=2211.0)

## Topics

### AI Editing Tool Updates

  - [New AI shortcuts tool accessible in drafts for broad adjustments](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=595.0)
  - [Provides diffs to review changes before accepting](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=630.0)
  - [Best used at brief stage or as replacement for feedback workflow](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=693.0)
  - [May struggle with very long writing guidelines; prioritize \~15 key instructions](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=1487.0)

### Image Generation Workflows

  - [Stock cover image workflow using Unsplash available](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=129.0)
  - [Illustrated cover image pipeline exists for some clients](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=206.0)
  - [Abyssal tool can remix existing brand assets for custom graphics](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=323.0)

### CMS Integrations

  - [Webflow publishing output exists in some grids](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=1944.0)
  - [Working on integrations for major CMSs like Sanity, Strapi, Contentful](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=1030.0)
  - [Custom integrations (e.g. KeyStatic/GitHub) more complex but possible](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=1091.0)
  - [Meta descriptions, titles, slugs to be investigated for output](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=1952.0)

### Atlas UI Feedback

  - [Request for more color and visual differentiation in interface](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=2089.0)
  - [Will be considered for future UX improvements](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=2167.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Run at least one full workflow end-to-end today to identify blockers](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=2225.0)
  - [File tickets for any issues encountered in Atlas](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=2211.0)
  - [Experiment with AI editing tool and report problems](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=511.0)
  - [Save latest AirOps artifacts locally as backup](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=2672.0)
  - [Investigate missing meta description/SEO outputs](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=1952.0)
  - [Continue rapid development and bug fixes for Atlas](https://fathom.video/share/PP7tZWm_KgYj7Qu75dcSyxipjzTV3S1P?tab=summary&timestamp=2764.0)
